title: HMI Session Lock Bypass — Long Idle Operator Consoles

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 2.5.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 2.5 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.20: HMI Session Lock Bypass — Long Idle Operator Consoles.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.20' running on index=hmi_audit sourcetype="winlogon" on HMI thin clients, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Windows TA or HMI vendor audit forwarder
spl:
index=hmi_audit (sourcetype="winlogon" OR sourcetype="hmi:session") earliest=-7d
| eval action=coalesce(EventCode, event_type)
| transaction user host maxspan=8h maxpause=15m
| eval duration_min=round(duration/60,1)
| where duration_min>240 AND mvcount(action)=1
| table starttime, endtime, user, host, duration_min
description: Measures continuous HMI operator sessions without screen-lock events to evidence session lock policy effectiveness.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes hmi_audit and sourcetypes hmi:session, winlogon. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Enforce GPO screen lock on HMI OS; (2) reduce `maxpause` per plant standard; (3) alert on transactions > policy; (4) physical walk-through for exceptions. Indexes required: `index=hmi_audit`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=hmi:session`, `sourcetype=winlogon`.
visualization: Timeline, Table, Single value (long sessions). Key fields: `action`, `duration_min`, `endtime`, `host`, `starttime`, `user`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Windows TA or HMI vendor audit forwarder.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=hmi_audit` `sourcetype="winlogon"` on HMI thin clients.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enforce GPO screen lock on HMI OS; (2) reduce `maxpause` per plant standard; (3) alert on transactions > policy; (4) physical walk-through for exceptions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hmi_audit (sourcetype="winlogon" OR sourcetype="hmi:session") earliest=-7d
| eval action=coalesce(EventCode, event_type)
| transaction user host maxspan=8h maxpause=15m
| eval duration_min=round(duration/60,1)
| where duration_min>240 AND mvcount(action)=1
| table starttime, endtime, user, host, duration_min
```

Understanding this SPL

**HMI Session Lock Bypass — Long Idle Operator Consoles (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.5)** — Measures continuous HMI operator sessions without screen-lock events to evidence session lock policy effectiveness.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hmi_audit` `sourcetype="winlogon"` on HMI thin clients. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Windows TA or HMI vendor audit forwarder. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hmi_audit; **sourcetype**: winlogon, hmi:session. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hmi_audit, sourcetype="winlogon", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **action** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Groups related events into transactions — prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **duration_min** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where duration_min>240 AND mvcount(action)=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: action, duration_min, endtime, host, starttime, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline, Table, Single value (long sessions).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for measures continuous hmi operator sessions without screen-lock events to evidence session lock policy effectiveness. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
